prep.summary: Align your preparation with the April 2nd meeting with Thomas—focusing on mitigating his friction with Larry Abel and Dan—and connect this to the May 28th session with Larry regarding ServiceNow POC limitations. Address the ongoing need for Thomas to partner tightly with Mike Halleck—avoiding intermediaries—while establishing clear business-value metrics for the Policy Summary database.
tasks: Counsel Thomas on engaging Dan directly with technical facts to counter negative performance perceptions.
Verify Thomas has been successfully integrated into all relevant solutioning meetings with Mike Halleck.
Review the completed documentation demonstrating the scalability value of the Policy Summary database.
Resolve the service monitoring fragmentation with Larry Abel by aligning ServiceNow evaluation with internal architectures.
prep.title: Thomas - Monthly 1on1

prep.summary: Review your June 1st meeting with Praveena—which centered on mid-year performance calibration, Matt's transition plan, and managing Amit's workload capacity. Finalizing the Product and Services initiative by the end of Q2 remains a top priority—requiring immediate team alignment. No work notes exist for your last meeting with John—requiring you to pull those updates from other sources.
tasks: Review the mid-year performance calibration spreadsheet—securing final approvals before the deadline.
Finalize Matt's transition plan—demanding 100% dedication through June 15th to hand off critical Policy Mod and DNS projects.
Develop a backup plan to address Amit's capacity concerns—improving visibility for stakeholders.
Request a direct update on John's recent initiatives—remedying the lack of recorded work notes in your Drive folder.
prep.title: Bi-weekly with Praveena

prep.summary: You discussed the Conversational Quote App initiative with Suresh on June 12—focusing on integrating TCS's demo with internal APIs and establishing a tight working group. No individual meeting note with Ramesh exists in your files—though he is named as a critical resource for this same quoting initiative. You must align these efforts during your sync—driving clarity on TCS's progress and Ramesh's immediate deliverables.
tasks: Review the TCS feasibility study timeline for the conversational quoting application.
Confirm Ramesh's availability and specific tasks within the newly formed working group.
Coordinate with Chuck Monro regarding tool access and vendor bottlenecks.
Establish the weekly working group cadence with Casey, Ramesh, Seema, and Mark.
prep.title: Monthly sync with Suresh

prep.summary: Your recent initiatives focus on eliminating process monitoring blind spots across P&C and Lifeco operations—driving a consolidated push toward a unified Process Health Scorecard. You are navigating a fragmented tool landscape—evaluating Celonis for real-time Kafka event ingestion, Scan AI for manual desktop flows, and ServiceNow to avoid high-cost vendor lock-in. Technical modernizations—including the Apache Flink real-time pipeline targeting a Q3 2026 production deployment and the 2027 Lifeco ISCO advisory model launch—will require clear architectural guardrails to prevent redundant platform spend.
tasks: Finalize the Enterprise Architecture position paper regarding holistic process observability—specifically aligning Celonis, ScanAI, and the existing Beam/Snowflake infrastructure.
Establish operational guardrails to address tool overlap among ServiceNow, Celonis, and Scan AI before procurement contracts are signed.
Revise the ServiceNow POC presentation deck for Dan Griffiths—moving directly from slide 5 to 9 and hiding non-essential slides.
Coordinate with Zahid and Andy to socialize the unified P&C process health strategy and initiate a weekly stakeholder meeting cadence.
prep.title: P&C IT EMG Planning Session
